Annotation

Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us
The patient shows elevated creatinine, proteinuria, glucosuria, ketonuria, and bilirubinuria, with low urine specific gravity and low HbA1c. These findings suggest impaired renal function (possible chronic kidney disease), abnormal urinary findings (suggesting possible nephrotic syndrome or tubular dysfunction), and possible transient hyperglycemia or renal glycosuria. Urine appearance (cloudy, brown, ammoniacal) may indicate underlying urinary tract pathology. Clinical correlation and further nephrological and metabolic evaluation are recommended.
